dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

- **Legal name:** RMP Plumbing LLC (VERIFIED — consistent across website, BBB, Yelp, BuildZoom). The "RMP" almost certainly nods to the Texas "Responsible Master Plumber" designation, a common naming convention in the trade (UNVERIFIED interpretation).
- **Vertical:** Residential/light-commercial plumbing — water heaters, whole-home repipe, gas line repair, drain/sewer, water filtration, plus some HVAC and gas-appliance/generator work (VERIFIED via site service pages).
- **Service area:** Cypress, Katy, Tomball and greater West/NW Houston (VERIFIED via site). The seed's 77063/77095 zips are both associated, but the picture is messier than the seed implies — three distinct addresses surface across sources: **4003 Waldo St, Houston 77063** (site footer + BBB + directories), **16630 Stoneside Dr, Houston 77095** (Yelp + Bradford White), and **7722 Shavano Ln, Cypress 77433** (one directory). This shifting-address pattern is a classic small/home-based owner-operator signal (UNVERIFIED reconciliation — confirm on Comptroller).
- **Texas entity status + formation date:** Not confirmed this session — the Comptroller account-status search is a POST form and could not be queried by URL. BBB file opened **2/26/2021**; one directory claims "established 2017"; earliest permit on record is **10/17/2019** (Pearland gas test, via BuildZoom). Best estimate: founded ~2017–2019, formalized by 2021 (UNVERIFIED — flag for live check).
- **License + status:** Site claims **Texas Master Plumber License #43398**. Caveat: TX Master Plumber numbers normally carry an "M" prefix, so the bare "#43398" needs board confirmation. BuildZoom separately shows a **Plumber Contractor Registration (REG08977) as inactive/cancelled** and an Arizona specialty license as cancelled. Net: a master license is claimed but **NOT independently verified on TSBPE this session** (UNVERIFIED — material diligence flag).
- **Active confirmation:** Website is **live** (Squarespace, multi-page, real content) (VERIFIED). Phone **(713) 261-3006** and a separate SMS line **(346) 280-9028** are published (VERIFIED present; not test-dialed).

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

- **Google Business Profile:** **Review count and rating could not be confirmed this session** — search engines did not surface the GBP panel and Yelp/BBB were the only review sources that resolved. This is the single most important missing reputation metric and is the #1 live-check item (UNVERIFIED — do not assume).
- **Yelp:** **13 reviews, ~4.0 stars**, 40 photos, listing at 16630 Stoneside Dr (VERIFIED count via Yelp page title + corroborating search; the seed's "Yelp 13 reviews" claim is CONFIRMED).
- **Facebook (facebook.com/plumbercypress):** **71 likes/followers, 0 page ratings** (VERIFIED; seed "FB 71 likes" CONFIRMED).
- **Instagram (@rmp_plumbing):** **~667 followers** (VERIFIED; seed "~667" CONFIRMED) — their largest single audience and clear demand-generation channel.
- **Angi:** rated **4.4/5** with a small review volume (UNVERIFIED exact count; Angi page returned 403).
- **BBB:** **Not accredited, "Not Rated"** (insufficient info), one review visible from "Filko A" — almost certainly owner Filip (VERIFIED no rating; seed "BBB thin/no rating" CONFIRMED).
- **Review themes — praise (proof the service is worth scaling):** punctual, professional, thorough diagnosis-before-work, fixed problems other plumbers couldn't, honest/knowledgeable techs (named tech "George" recurs). This is exactly the quality base a conversion system can amplify.
- **Review themes — complaints (pain points):** at least one serious callback failure (a repaired pipe re-burst within a week and the company allegedly went unresponsive and declined damages) and pricing complaints around the Arctic-freeze emergency period. Signals: thin after-the-sale follow-up and no systematized warranty/communication loop — both fixable with SMS automation.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

- **Website:** rmp-plumbing.com on **Squarespace** (VERIFIED — `images.squarespace-cdn.com` artifacts). Multi-page, reasonably content-rich (services, service areas, about, testimonials), better than a bare UENI/templated one-pager. Mobile experience appears standard Squarespace-responsive (UNVERIFIED — confirm live).
- **Booking:** This is the core leak. The site routes to an **external Dispatch.me booking link** (`customer.dispatch.me/booking?account_id=37&org_id=568146`) with the instruction "pick any 3 convenient dates and times… and we'll take care of the rest." That is a **request-three-slots, human-follows-up model — NOT instant-confirm online booking** (VERIFIED). So despite having a scheduling tool, the customer never leaves with a confirmed appointment; the booking is gated behind a callback. Phone and SMS are pushed as primary.
- **Payment:** Cards accepted (Visa/MC/Discover/Amex) plus financing for select purchases (VERIFIED on site) — better than cash/Zelle-only operators. No evidence of online pay/deposit at booking.
- **SMS/email automation:** A published SMS number exists, but **no evidence of automated confirmations, reminders, or follow-up sequences** — the Dispatch flow and a callback-pipe-burst complaint both point to manual, human-dependent follow-up (UNVERIFIED negative — confirm live).
- **Lead-capture quality:** Demand is generated socially (667 IG followers, active brand) but capture is weak — testimonials are **5 hand-typed static quotes**, not a live Google-reviews widget, so social proof at the decision point is thin and unverifiable to a buyer.
- **SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** **The booking handoff.** Interested buyers are pushed from a polished site into an off-brand "submit 3 preferred times and wait for us to call" flow with no instant confirmation and no automated follow-up. High-intent plumbing demand (often emergency) is time-sensitive — every minute of callback lag is a job lost to the competitor who answers/books first. This is the exact 7%→30.8% booking-conversion problem Zapp solved for Royal Pawz.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads / LSAs:** **None detected** — no sponsored listings or Local Service Ads surfaced (UNVERIFIED negative; confirm live).
- **Meta Ad Library:** No active or inactive ads found for the brand/page this session (UNVERIFIED — Ad Library fetch was blocked; confirm live).
- **Platform dependency:** Present on Yelp, Angi, BBB, BuildZoom and several auto-aggregated directories, but no evidence of paid Yelp/Angi/Thumbtack/HomeAdvisor lead-buying (UNVERIFIED).
- **Pixels/tracking:** Not confirmed — inspect site source live for Meta Pixel / Google tag. The absence of ads suggests conversion tracking is likely minimal or absent.
- **SEO footprint:** Effectively **invisible for competitive core terms.** They do not appear in the top organic results for "best plumber Cypress/Katy"; those slots are owned by SEO-heavy incumbents (Mr. Potty/Obsta, 24/7 Plumbing Co., Joe the Plumber, Daniels). RMP's reach is **social-first (Instagram) rather than search-first** — meaning they're generating brand demand but not capturing high-intent search demand. The seed's "no heavy SEO/ads, social-first owner-operator" claim is CONFIRMED.

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Members (owners):** **Filip Nikolov Arssenov** and **Vladimir Simeonov Angelov**, both listed as LLC members on BuildZoom (UNVERIFIED — single source; reconcile with Comptroller/TSBPE). The BBB review byline "Filko A" corroborates Filip's involvement.
- **Personality / personal presence:** Bulgarian/Eastern-European ownership — a likely **bilingual angle** (and a potential immigrant-built-business / craftsmanship story) worth surfacing in a pitch (UNVERIFIED). "George" in testimonials is a field technician, not an owner.
- **Direct contact:** Business line (713) 261-3006, SMS (346) 280-9028, info@rmp-plumbing.com. No LinkedIn or personal owner profile located this session.
- **Engagement signals:** Owner personally engages on review platforms (the "Filko A" BBB review) and runs an active Instagram — both signal an owner who is reachable and already invested in the brand's online presence, which is a good fit for a done-for-you growth partner.

## 6. Competitive landscape

West/NW Houston plumbing is crowded and SEO-mature. Representative incumbents:
- **24/7 Plumbing Co., LP** — A+ BBB-accredited, strong brand, broad service-area SEO pages.
- **Mr. Potty / Obsta Plumbing** — family-owned since 2010, heavy location-page SEO (Cypress/Katy landing pages).
- **Joe the Plumber / Daniels Plumbing** — 20+ years, deep review volume and local search dominance.

**What incumbents have that RMP lacks:** large Google review counts, top organic + map-pack rankings, paid LSAs with the Google Guaranteed badge, and in many cases real online booking. **Where RMP can realistically win:** it already has a genuine quality reputation (4.0 Yelp / 4.4 Angi with authentic praise) and an engaged 667-follower Instagram audience that the bigger, faceless incumbents don't cultivate. RMP doesn't need to out-SEO 20-year incumbents overnight — it needs to **convert the demand it already creates** (social + word-of-mouth + map views) into booked jobs via instant booking, visible review proof, and SMS follow-up. That's a faster, cheaper lever than a multi-year SEO war.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket size:** Residential plumbing service calls commonly run **$300–$700**, water-heater replacement **$1,500–$3,500**, repipe/sewer/gas jobs **$3,000–$10,000+** (UNVERIFIED — industry-typical ranges). A blended booked-job average of **~$600–$900** is reasonable for this mix.
- **Paid-search economics (West Houston):** Plumbing PPC clicks run roughly **$5–$30+**, with emergency terms ("emergency plumber near me") **$30–$50+** per click (VERIFIED directionally from Houston agency sources). At ~5–10% lead-to-job, blended **CAC commonly lands ~$150–$400 per booked job**; at a ~$700 ticket a well-run funnel clears **3–5x ROAS** — the same range Zapp hit for Royal Pawz.
- **Seasonality:** Strong winter spikes (freeze events drive emergency demand — note the Arctic-freeze pricing complaints), summer water-heater and slab-leak demand; relatively steady year-round vs. seasonal trades.
- **Affluent-geo framing:** Target the **Memorial / Briar Forest / Energy Corridor** residential corridor (high-ticket repipes, tankless installs, whole-home filtration) rather than the lower-income Westchase/Royal Oaks pocket — higher average tickets and better paid-search ROI.
- **Royal-Pawz-style lift, in their dollars (simple math):** Assume RMP gets ~120 booking-intent actions/month (calls + site visits + Dispatch starts) and currently converts ~10% to booked jobs = ~12 jobs. At a $700 avg ticket that's **~$8,400/mo**. Double the booking rate to ~20% (Royal Pawz went 7%→30.8%, so 2x is conservative) = ~24 jobs = **~$16,800/mo** — a **~$8,400/mo incremental gain (~$100k/yr)** from conversion alone, before adding any new paid traffic. Layer in West-Houston Google/LSA spend at 4x ROAS and the upside compounds. (Illustrative — inputs UNVERIFIED; validate intent volume in diagnostic.)

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why them / why now:** A real, well-reviewed, owner-operated plumber that is already creating demand (667 IG followers, authentic 4.0/4.4 reputation) but **leaking it at the booking handoff** — a polished Squarespace site dumping buyers into a "give us 3 times and wait for a callback" Dispatch flow, static testimonials instead of live review proof, and zero paid acquisition. That's a textbook conversion-infrastructure gap, not a quality problem.
- **The lead gap:** No instant online booking, no automated SMS/email follow-up, no visible/verifiable review proof at the decision point, no Google Ads/LSA presence capturing high-intent search.
- **Recommended Zapp tier:** **Revenue Diagnostic** first. Rationale: there's a meaningful diligence stack to clear before a Build — confirm Google review count/rating, verify the Master Plumber license on TSBPE (the "M"-prefix anomaly + cancelled registrations are real flags), confirm entity status, and reconcile three business addresses. The diagnostic de-risks all of that and quantifies real booking-intent volume; a Build follows naturally once verified.
- **Fit score rationale (68/100):** Strong on owner-operator + reachable owner + thin conversion infra + high-ticket/recurring vertical + West Houston affluent geo. Held back from higher by (a) they already have *some* infrastructure (Squarespace + Dispatch + card payments), so they're not a blank slate; (b) modest current demand footprint (13 Yelp, 71 FB); and (c) unresolved license/entity diligence flags. Not an early "invisible" lead, but not an established 100+-review brand either — squarely a mid-stage convert-what-you-have play.
- **Diligence flags:** (1) Master Plumber #43398 unverified + cancelled contractor registration on BuildZoom; (2) three different business addresses; (3) at least one serious unresponsive-callback complaint (operational follow-through risk); (4) Google review count entirely unconfirmed — could move the fit score up or down materially.

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & Verification

- **Legal name:** Houston Strong Roofing Company, LLC (VERIFIED — appears as the LLC name on BBB profile and the company's own site footer).
- **Vertical:** Residential roofing — roof replacement, repair, inspections, maintenance, storm-damage/insurance claims; materials span asphalt shingle, metal, and tile; also offers seamless gutters (VERIFIED via website + Facebook).
- **Service area:** Headquartered in Cypress 77429; markets to Cypress, The Woodlands, and Spring (VERIFIED, website tagline). Draft note's "Memorial / Spring Branch / Katy" is only partially supported — the site's own stated footprint is Cypress / The Woodlands / Spring, i.e. NORTHWEST Houston, not the Memorial/Energy-Corridor west side (UNVERIFIED for Memorial/Katy/Spring Branch; treat as aspirational, not confirmed).
- **Physical address:** 15705 Jarvis Rd, Cypress, TX 77429 (VERIFIED — BBB, website, Yelp, multiple aggregators agree). A secondary Yelp listing shows 6830 N Eldridge Pkwy, Ste 311, Houston 77041 (UNVERIFIED secondary location / possible duplicate or older listing). Owner states on Nextdoor the office is in/near the Laurel Creek subdivision in Cypress (VERIFIED, owner's own words).
- **Texas entity status:** Business started **10/7/2013** per BBB; "Years in Business: 12"; entity type listed as Corporation/LLC (VERIFIED date via BBB, an authoritative aggregator; **direct Texas Comptroller / SOS franchise-tax "right to transact" status could NOT be pulled this session** — the Comptroller search requires interactive form submission and the open-data endpoint refused connection. Mark entity-active status UNVERIFIED pending a live Comptroller check).
- **License + status:** **Texas does NOT license roofing contractors at the state level** — TDLR does not administer roofer licensure, so there is no state license to verify (VERIFIED, structural fact). The relevant voluntary credential is RCAT (Roofing Contractors Association of Texas). Could not confirm whether Houston Strong holds an RCAT license this session (RCAT directory requires interactive search) — **UNVERIFIED**. BBB Accredited since 3/1/2017 with an **A+ rating** (VERIFIED, BBB).
- **Active confirmation:** Site is **live** (houstonstrongroofing.com loads; BBB also lists houstrongroofing.com as an alternate/legacy domain). Phones (832) 271-7783 and (832) 801-9608 both appear across the site, BBB, and Nextdoor and are consistent (VERIFIED as published; not test-dialed this session). Email Daron@houstonstrongroofing.com confirmed (VERIFIED). Business is demonstrably active — Google reviews flow through **June 2026** (VERIFIED via Trustindex feed).

**Bottom line: this is an established, 12-year-old, BBB-A+ family brand — NOT an early-stage operator. The draft "early-stage / 12 reviews" framing is a misread (the 12 was the Yelp count).**

## 2. Reputation & Demand Proof

- **Google:** Roughly **213–239 reviews at ~4.9–5.0 stars** (VERIFIED range — multiple aggregators converge: LocallyFind 213 @ 4.9; Trustindex feed ~238 @ 5.0 with 242 five-star / 2 three-star / 2 one-star; website claims 237; one source cites ~296 "happy customers"). Exact live count needs a browser check, but the brand clearly sits in the **200+ Google-review tier at a near-perfect rating** (VERIFIED directionally). GBP claim status not directly confirmable via fetch — **UNVERIFIED** (almost certainly claimed given the volume and review velocity).
- **Yelp:** Two listings exist. Cypress listing (15705 Jarvis Rd): **~12 reviews**, photos updated May 2026 (VERIFIED). Houston listing (6830 N Eldridge Pkwy): **~11 reviews** (VERIFIED). Yelp is a weak channel for them — low double digits — consistent with the draft note's "Yelp 12."
- **Facebook:** **96% recommend, 22 reviews** (VERIFIED — matches draft note; page @houstrongroofing).
- **BBB:** A+ Accredited since 2017; complaint count not disclosed in the excerpt (UNVERIFIED count, but no complaint pattern surfaced).
- **Nextdoor:** Active page with neighbor recommendations (VERIFIED qualitatively; exact recommendation count not pulled).
- **Review themes — PRAISE (proof the service is worth scaling):** professionalism, responsiveness, communication, fast turnaround, thorough cleanup, honesty, quality product + "superior warranty," hands-on owner involvement (Daron, Sarah, and PM Chris repeatedly named). Several mention hail/storm re-roofs handled end-to-end with the insurance company (VERIFIED themes across Nextdoor, FB, BBB, Yelp).
- **Review themes — COMPLAINTS / pain points:** essentially none surfaced — only 2 one-star and 2 three-star in a ~240-review Google set. No recurring complaint theme found this session (VERIFIED as "no pattern detected"). The demand and trust proof is strong; the constraint is volume/conversion, not reputation.

## 3. Current Funnel Teardown (THE CORE)

- **Website:** houstonstrongroofing.com — built on **WordPress** (PHP, Cloudflare CDN, Google Fonts API detected) (VERIFIED via builtwith-style signal). This is an agency/custom WordPress site, not a DIY UENI/Wix template — reasonably polished, with clear "GET YOUR FREE ESTIMATE" CTAs throughout. No visible web-designer/agency credit in the footer.
- **Booking:** **NO online booking / NO scheduler / NO instant-quote calculator.** The conversion path is a single "Get Your Free Estimate" **contact form** ("Fill out the below form and we'll respond back shortly") plus click-to-call and email (VERIFIED). This is **form-and-phone-only** — the prospect must wait for a human callback; there is no self-serve "pick a time" path.
- **Payment:** No online payment. Financing is offered via a **Wells Fargo** partnership with "flexible payment plans" (VERIFIED). Payment is offline/job-based (normal for roofing).
- **Follow-up automation:** **No SMS or email automation detected.** No live-chat widget, no text-quote option, no visible chat/automation tooling on the site (VERIFIED — none found). Lead handling appears manual (owner replies personally per draft note and review tone).
- **Lead-capture quality:** Mediocre-to-weak. A single generic estimate form with no booking, no instant scheduling, no automated speed-to-lead. For a storm/insurance-driven business where the first responder usually wins the job, a callback-only form is a real leak.
- **Tracking:** No Meta/Google pixel could be confirmed via fetch (rendered JS not captured) — **UNVERIFIED**; needs a live network/console check.
- **SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** **No instant online booking + no automated speed-to-lead (SMS) follow-up.** A 200-plus-review, 4.9-star brand is sending all its hard-won traffic and referrals into a passive "fill out a form and wait for a callback" funnel. Every after-hours storm visitor, every mobile lead, and every "I'll call three roofers" shopper who doesn't get an instant response is a leaked, high-ticket ($9k+) job. This is the exact gap Zapp closes (booking + Twilio SMS follow-up).

## 4. Acquisition & Ad Presence

- **Google Ads:** Draft note claims they "ALREADY RUN ADS." Could **not independently confirm a live Google Ads campaign** this session (no sponsored result captured for "roofing Cypress") — **UNVERIFIED**. Needs a live SERP check for branded + non-branded terms.
- **Meta Ad Library:** Could not load the Ad Library via fetch (connection closed) — **active-ad status UNVERIFIED**. Needs a direct Ad Library check for Page "Houston Strong Roofing Company."
- **Platform dependency:** Light. Present on Yelp (low review counts, no Yelp-Ads "Portfolio" signal confirmed), Nextdoor, BBB, and several auto-generated directories (LocallyFind, Directorii, Trustanalytica, ZoomInfo). No evidence of heavy Angi/Thumbtack/HomeAdvisor reliance — they appear to run primarily on **organic reviews + referrals + GBP** (VERIFIED directionally).
- **SEO footprint:** Strong brand/GBP presence (ranks for their own name and shows up in Cypress roofing directory roundups), but no evidence captured that they dominate competitive non-branded terms like "roof replacement Cypress" / "roofers Cypress TX" against the incumbents below (UNVERIFIED for non-branded rankings — needs a live rank check). The 200+ Google reviews almost certainly give them strong Map-pack pull in 77429.

**Net: acquisition appears review/referral-led with thin (or unconfirmed) paid infrastructure. If they ARE running ads, those ads point at a form-only WordPress page — meaning ad spend is being throttled by the funnel, not the traffic. That is the strongest possible setup for a conversion-system pitch.**

## 5. Owner / Decision-Maker

- **Owners:** **Daron Burt (Owner) and Sarah Burt** — husband-and-wife, family-owned (VERIFIED — BBB lists Daron Burt as Owner; Nextdoor and FB reviews name Daron & Sarah; owner's Nextdoor bio: "We are a small, family owned company. We live In Laurel Creek Subdivision and our office is just down the street").
- **Founder story:** Daron started as a roofing laborer in **2005**, founded Houston Strong Roofing in **2013**; company motto "impact over income," civic "Houston Strong" framing (VERIFIED via search of company narrative).
- **Direct contact:** Daron@houstonstrongroofing.com; (832) 801-9608 (the number the owner publishes on Nextdoor) and (832) 271-7783 (VERIFIED).
- **LinkedIn:** Daron Burt has a LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/daron-burt-2a3619215) listing him at Houston Strong Roofing Company LLC (VERIFIED exists; profile detail not extractable this session).
- **Personality / outreach signals:** Hands-on, personally responsive owner (named individually in dozens of reviews), values-driven ("impact over income"), genuinely small/local (lives in the neighborhood he serves). Project manager "Chris" and crew members (Trey, Josh, Brandon, Gregg) named positively. **Family-owned + woman-co-owned (Sarah) + hyper-local owner-operator** are all strong rapport angles. The personal, name-on-the-door responsiveness is exactly the profile that adopts an operator-run acquisition system well — but also means he likely guards brand voice, so the pitch should respect the existing 4.9-star reputation.

## 6. Competitive Landscape (NW Houston / Cypress 77429 roofing)

- **Mighty Dog Roofing of Northwest Houston** (13320 Telge Rd, Cypress 77429) — national franchise with brand polish, BBB A+ Accredited, Owens Corning Platinum-style credentialing, multi-service (roofing/gutters/siding/windows), GuildQuality + Angi review syndication (VERIFIED exists). Has franchise marketing muscle and likely paid lead-gen infrastructure Houston Strong lacks.
- **Texas Strong Roofers** (texasstrongroofers.com) — veteran-owned, name-collision competitor targeting the same "strong" branding and Houston service areas (VERIFIED exists). A direct brand-confusion + SEO competitor.

  - If they ARE buying Google Ads at $150–$200 CPL, halving wasted clicks by fixing the landing page/booking flow is worth thousands per month on its own.

This is the single strongest demotion signal — review volume is large and mature, not nascent.

**Demand proof:** Strong, well-established, multi-channel. The business does not lack demand or reputation — undercutting the core Zapp thesis (which monetizes turning latent demand into booked jobs for under-marketed operators).

## 3. Current Funnel Teardown (THE CORE)

**Website:** myroofimprovement.com — **WordPress** (wp-content artifacts), purpose-built marketing site with dozens of SEO landing pages (per-service + per-city: /houston/, /roofing-houston/, /roof-replacement-in-houston/, /roof-repair-company-in-houston/, etc.). This is a deliberately SEO-architected site, not a templated one-pager. There is ALSO a sprawl of secondary/auto-generated directory profiles (nicepage.io, houstononline.us, about.me, location.com, semfirms.com) — low-quality citation spam, not the primary funnel.

**Mobile / template artifacts:** Modern WordPress build; no obvious cheap-template tells (UENI/Wix/GoDaddy) found. Mobile experience to be confirmed live.

**Booking / lead capture:** This is where the seed's thesis collapses. They run a **comparatively sophisticated lead funnel**:
- A **4-step progressive contact form** (with a 25% completion progress bar) capturing property type, owner status, service type, address, stories, square-footage ranges, best-time-to-call, and project notes.
- A **separate streamlined "Schedule a Call" form** with discount-qualification fields (military / first responder / nurse / teacher / senior).

**What's MISSING:** No real-time **calendar booking** (you can't self-book a slot — it's "request a callback"). No **live chat**. **SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** **Speed-to-lead / after-hours capture.** A multi-step form plus a "best time to call" field plus three different phone numbers implies **human, business-hours follow-up** (M-F 8-6, closed weekends). Roofing storm-damage leads are urgent and impulsive; the homeowner who fills the form Saturday night and gets no instant text/booking confirmation can be intercepted by an incumbent with instant booking. 1. **Amstill Roofing** — the dominant incumbent for Energy Corridor/Memorial/Briar Forest/Hedwig Village. **50+ years**, GAF Master Elite, **5,000+ reviews.** Out-classes JC&C on review volume and brand age by an order of magnitude.
2. **Greater Houston Roofing** — explicitly targets "west of Memorial Villages + Energy Corridor"; strong local SEO.
3. **Moss Roofing / Bear Brothers / Rose Roofing / All Out Roofing** — all run free-inspection funnels and contest the same Memorial/Energy-Corridor affluent ZIPs.

**What JC&C already matches:** instant-quote tool, financing, GAF Master Elite, strong reviews, multi-page SEO. **Where it trails:** raw review volume vs. Amstill, and decades of brand equity. **Where JC&C can realistically win:** the bilingual/Latino-family-owned + owner-on-site niche, and storm-claim responsiveness — but these are positioning plays, not funnel-build plays.

## 7. Vertical Economics & ROI Model

- **Ticket size:** Roof **replacement** in Houston typically **$9,000–$30,000+** (large/complex Memorial & Energy Corridor homes skew to $20k–$45k+); repairs $400–$2,500.
- **Paid-search economics:** Roofing is one of the **most expensive PPC verticals** — Houston roofing keyword CPCs often **$15–$40+/click**, with cost-per-acquired-lead frequently **$80–$250** and booked-job CAC commonly **$500–$1,500+**. Even so, on a $15k–$25k replacement, a healthy ROAS is very achievable — which is exactly why JC&C already invests in SEO/instant-quote infrastructure.
- **Seasonality:** Storm/insurance-claim spikes (Houston hail/wind season spring–early summer; hurricane season Jun–Nov; the site name-checks Hurricane Beryl). Demand is event-driven and urgent → speed-to-lead matters more here than in steady-state services.
- **Royal-Pawz-style lift, applied to THEIR dollars:** A grooming-style "7%→30%" green-field jump does **not** translate — JC&C already converts well and isn't conversion-starved. The only honest math is a marginal speed-to-lead recovery: if they take, say, ~40 web leads/month and currently miss ~15% of after-hours leads to faster competitors, recovering even 3–4 of those into a single extra $18k replacement is ~$18k–$70k of recovered annual revenue. That is a **conversion-rate-optimization** value story, not a 50x-MRR transformation story — and JC&C is sophisticated enough to question whether they need an outside operator to add an SMS auto-responder.

**Why-them-why-now (honest version):** "You already own demand, reviews, SEO, and an instant-quote tool — but your intake still routes everything to a business-hours callback. The only money on the table is speed-to-lead: instant SMS confirmation + self-serve calendar booking so the Saturday-night storm lead books with you instead of Amstill." That is a **thin, optimization-grade** hook.

**The lead gap:** Not a missing funnel — a missing **automated, after-hours, instant-response/booking layer** on top of an already-good funnel.

**Recommended Zapp tier:** **Not a fit** for the flagship done-for-you funnel build. If pursued at all, only a small **Revenue Diagnostic** to quantify speed-to-lead leakage — and even that competes against the client's own competence.

**Fit score rationale (22/100):** Fails most Zapp ICP criteria — they are NOT under-marketed (200+ Google reviews, deep SEO), NOT missing conversion infra (multi-step funnel + Roofr instant quote + financing), and the vertical's high CAC + high competence make the "we'll build and run your acquisition" pitch a hard sell. **Diligence flags:** (a) Seed mislabeled this as early-stage/2025-build — verify nothing else in the prospecting list shares that error pattern; (b) Comptroller franchise-tax active-status not confirmed; (c) paid-ad presence (Meta/Google) genuinely unconfirmed due to fetch blocks — could move the needle slightly if they're spending and under-converting, but the funnel is already strong; (d) review counts are aggregator-derived, not read live off Google/Yelp.

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

- **Legal/brand name:** Modern Air of Katy; the underlying entity is referenced as a Texas LLC ("Modern Heating and Air Conditioning L.L.C." per the website's schema/org data and BBB's "Limited Liability Company" structure). (UNVERIFIED — exact registered legal name not confirmed via Texas SOS/Comptroller this session; Comptroller and data.texas.gov search interfaces did not return a populated record.)
- **Vertical:** HVAC — residential & light-commercial AC repair/install/replacement, heating/furnace repair, maintenance plans, ductwork/zoning, indoor air quality. (VERIFIED — site + multiple review platforms.)
- **Service area (stated):** Based/HQ in Spring Branch West (1525 Wycliffe Dr, Houston, TX 77043), dispatching to Memorial & Memorial Villages, Briar Forest / Energy Corridor (77077), Katy, Cinco Ranch, Grand Lakes, Copperfield, Cypress, Sugar Land, and general West Houston. Note: the seed's "Westchase/Royal Oaks" pocket also appears in their listed coverage, but their own location pages explicitly target the affluent Memorial / Energy Corridor / Briar Forest corridor — the right ROI geo. (VERIFIED — modernairofkaty.com area pages + Westchase Houston Now directory.)
- **Owner:** Charles Guthrie, Jr. (VERIFIED — BBB lists "Mr. Charles Guthrie, Owner"; LinkedIn "Charles Guthrie, Jr. — Owner — Modern Air"; Instagram @charlesgmodern1; email charles@modernairofkaty.com in site schema.)
- **Business start date:** August 17, 2011 per BBB; "since 2011," founder with "30+ years industry experience." (VERIFIED via BBB; the "30+ years" is the owner's personal experience, not company age.)
- **Texas entity status + formation date:** (UNVERIFIED) — could not pull a Comptroller franchise-tax / SOS record this session; BBB's 2011 start date is the best available proxy. LIVE-CHECK REQUIRED.
- **License + status:** Site claims "licensed and insured" but publishes **no TACLB/ACR number**. I could NOT verify a TDLR ACR contractor license tied to Charles Guthrie / Modern Air of Katy. CAUTION: a web search surfaced "TACLB94688E" attached to **"Modern Air Mechanical LLC" (9807 Sagebud Ln, Houston 77089, 713-309-0502)** — a **different, unrelated** business. Do NOT attribute that license to this prospect. (License status: UNVERIFIED — flag.)
- **Active confirmation:** Site live (modernairofkaty.com, WordPress, recently published/relaunched ~May 2025). Second domain modernair-katy.com also resolves (likely an older/parallel site — verify it's not a stale duplicate diluting SEO). Phone 713-620-8900 listed consistently across all platforms. Recent reviews (through June 2026) confirm an actively operating business. (VERIFIED.)

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

- **Google:** ~142–145 reviews, ~4.9–5.0 stars (counts cited: 127, 132+, 142, 143, 145 across snapshots — converging on ~140s). (VERIFIED that volume is in the 140s and rating ~4.9; exact live count needs confirmation.) GBP claim status: not confirmed; Birdeye flags its mirror as "unclaimed," but the business clearly manages Google directly (replies to 100% of reviews within ~1 day per Trustindex).
- **Velocity signal (important):** Reviews are **concentrated in the last ~7–8 months (≈Nov 2025 → June 2026)**, not spread across 2011–2026. This indicates a **recent, deliberate reputation campaign** (likely HouseCall Pro / Trustindex review automation) that took them from near-zero to 140+ in well under a year. (VERIFIED — Trustindex/Birdeye date ranges.)
- **Yelp:** Profile exists (yelp.com/biz/modern-air-of-katy-houston) but returned 403; count/rating unconfirmed. (UNVERIFIED.)
- **Facebook:** Active page (facebook.com/modernairofkaty) posting blog/service content (posts seen Oct/Dec 2024, Jan 2025). Follower/recommendation count not extracted. (UNVERIFIED on counts.)
- **Nextdoor:** Own page with ~9 neighbor recommendations; owner posts content. (VERIFIED page exists; count UNVERIFIED beyond "~9.")
- **Angi / HomeAdvisor:** Profiles exist, both shown "5.0 overall." (VERIFIED profiles exist; small/low-volume.)
- **Birdeye / Trustindex / GuildQuality:** Birdeye mirrors 145 Google reviews (4.9); Trustindex widget embedded on their reviews page ("EXCELLENT, 142 reviews"); GuildQuality profile exists but has **0 reviews**. (VERIFIED.)
- **Review THEMES — praise (proof worth scaling):** Charles personally trusted; "honest, doesn't upsell," fast response ("fixed in 20–30 min"), fair/competitive pricing, sources warranty parts to save customers money, long-term repeat relationships (one customer 12 yrs / 4 installs). (VERIFIED — recurring across Birdeye/Trustindex/Angi.)
- **Complaints:** None surfaced in visible reviews — uniformly positive. (No negative theme found; BBB "Not Rated" only because file just opened Dec 2024, not due to complaints.)

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

- **Website:** modernairofkaty.com — **WordPress**, recently built/relaunched (schema datePublished 2025-05-18, modified 2026-05-10). Multi-page with dedicated location/service landing pages (Memorial, Energy Corridor, Katy, AC install, maintenance, zoning) — the seed's "heavily templated, freshly-built landing pages" is accurate about the SITE, even though the BUSINESS is 15 years old. Mobile experience not deeply tested (LIVE-CHECK).
- **Booking:** **Online booking EXISTS** — HouseCall Pro "Book Same-Day Service" widget, plus a basic contact form (Name/Phone/Email/Message) and prominent click-to-call. This is materially better than the typical Zapp prospect: they already have frictionless self-scheduling. (VERIFIED.)
- **Payment:** Online payment status not confirmed (HouseCall Pro supports it, but not verified live). (UNVERIFIED.)
- **Follow-up automation:** No SMS/chat widget detected on contact page. HouseCall Pro can do SMS reminders/follow-up but it's unconfirmed whether they run lead-nurture SMS for unbooked inquiries. (UNVERIFIED — likely a real gap.)
- **Lead-capture quality:** Decent — multiple pathways (booking widget, form, phone), 24/7 messaging, email charles@ in schema.
- **SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** Not the booking widget (they have one). The leak is **top-of-funnel demand capture, not on-site conversion** — there is **no detectable paid-search presence** driving qualified clicks into that already-good funnel, and they are **invisible vs SEO incumbents** for the money keywords in their target affluent geos. Secondarily: no detected SMS speed-to-lead / nurture on form fills, and a possible split-domain SEO issue (modernair-katy.com vs modernairofkaty.com). They've built the conversion machine and the reputation — they're just not feeding it enough traffic.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads:** No active Google Ads detected this session (Ads Transparency Center fetch blocked/404; no "Sponsored" placements surfaced in searches). (UNVERIFIED-negative — needs live Transparency Center check, but no evidence of active paid search.)
- **Meta Ad Library:** No ads found (library fetch failed; no advertiser surfaced). (UNVERIFIED-negative.)
- **Platform dependency:** Light Angi/HomeAdvisor presence and a Nextdoor page, but not heavily lead-gen-dependent; demand appears to come from organic reputation/referral, not paid platforms.
- **Pixels:** Not confirmed (WordPress + HouseCall Pro embed present; pixel audit is a LIVE-CHECK).
- **SEO footprint:** Has invested in location/service landing pages (good on-page intent), but is **not a dominant organic ranker** for core West-Houston HVAC terms — incumbents (Houston Air Pros 2,400+ reviews, Hage Energy ~210, Smart Air) outweigh them. Risk: two domains may be splitting authority.

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Name/role:** Charles Guthrie, Jr., Owner-operator and lead technician. (VERIFIED.)
- **Direct contact:** charles@modernairofkaty.com; 713-620-8900. (VERIFIED.)
- **Personal presence:** LinkedIn (charles-guthrie-jr-8982994a), Instagram @charlesgmodern1. (VERIFIED exist.)
- **Personality signals:** Highly engaged operator — personally named in nearly every review, replies to 100% of reviews within ~a day, posts content to Facebook/Nextdoor. Brand identity = "honest Charles who won't upsell." (VERIFIED.) This is a marketing-aware, hands-on owner who has already adopted modern tooling (HouseCall Pro, Trustindex, fresh WordPress site, review automation).
- **Family-/woman-owned/bilingual:** Described as "family-owned" in some listings; "Jr." suggests a family lineage. No bilingual or woman-owned angle confirmed. (Partly UNVERIFIED.)

## 6. Competitive landscape

- **Houston Air Pros (Energy Corridor):** 4.9★, **2,400+ Google reviews**, same-day repair, online booking, almost certainly running paid search. Dominant volume incumbent. (VERIFIED.)
- **Hage Energy (5773 Woodway, Energy Corridor):** ~208–217 reviews, 5.0★, 30+ yrs, Trane dealer, strong service-area SEO pages. (VERIFIED.)
- **Smart Air (Westpark):** family-owned, online booking, ~138 Yelp reviews, upfront pricing/same-day. (VERIFIED.)
- **What they have that Modern Air lacks:** Review VOLUME at incumbent scale (Houston Air Pros 17x more), entrenched organic rankings, and almost certainly active paid-search budgets capturing "AC repair near me" demand.
- **Where Modern Air can realistically win:** (a) Owner-trust differentiation ("Charles, honest, no upsell") is a premium-conversion story that paid landing pages can amplify; (b) they already convert well (real booking widget + 4.9★) so incremental paid traffic should convert efficiently; (c) tight geo-targeting on Memorial / Energy Corridor / Briar Forest where ticket sizes are high and the brand already has location pages — they don't need to out-review Houston Air Pros city-wide, just win the affluent micro-geos.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket size:** West-Houston residential HVAC — repair tickets ~$250–$700; full system replacement/install $7,000–$15,000+ (higher in Memorial/Energy Corridor with larger homes & premium systems). (UNVERIFIED industry-typical.)
- **Paid-search economics:** HVAC is a high-CPC vertical; "AC repair Houston" clicks commonly $15–$40+; cost-per-booked-call often $80–$250. A tuned landing page + speed-to-lead SMS can pull blended ROAS well above breakeven given the high install ticket. (UNVERIFIED industry-typical.)
- **Seasonality:** Strong summer (May–Sept cooling) demand spike in Houston + winter heating; an always-on paid + speed-to-lead system captures the summer surge they're sitting in right now (June). (VERIFIED-seasonal logic.)
- **Royal-Pawz-style lift math (illustrative, owner's dollars):** Suppose 30 net website leads/mo. If current on-site conversion is already decent (~25%) but they're starving the funnel, the bigger lever is volume + speed-to-lead. Add ~150 qualified paid clicks/mo at ~8% lead rate = ~12 extra leads; close ~35% = ~4 extra jobs/mo. At a blended $900 avg ticket (mix of repairs + the occasional install), that's ~$3,600/mo incremental, and a single install month can be $7k–$15k. Layer speed-to-lead SMS on existing form fills (recovering, say, 3 missed leads/mo → 1 job) and the system pays for itself well before a Royal-Pawz-scale multiple. The honest framing for Charles: he's already built the conversion engine and the 4.9★ trust — he's just not pouring enough targeted traffic into it.

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why-them / why-now:** Charles has done the hard part — fresh WordPress site, HouseCall Pro booking, 140+ five-star reviews built in ~7 months, 100% reply rate. He's a marketing-aware owner-operator missing only the demand-capture top of the funnel: no detectable paid search, invisible vs incumbents in his own affluent target geos, and (likely) no speed-to-lead SMS on inbound forms. It's June — peak cooling season — so traffic-to-booking is the immediate money lever.
- **Lead gap:** Demand acquisition (Google Ads → geo-targeted Memorial/Energy Corridor landing page → his existing booking flow → Twilio speed-to-lead SMS). The conversion infra is already ~80% there; Zapp's value is feeding and tightening it, not rebuilding it.
- **DEMOTION APPLIED:** Seed framed this as "early-stage / lead-gen shell / freshly-built." Reality: a 15-year-old established brand (since 2011), real owner-operator, 140+ reviews, real booking. The "freshly built" was only true of the WEBSITE. This LOWERS the early-stage fit — they're more sophisticated than the typical Royal-Pawz blank-slate prospect, and they already own the booking layer Zapp usually sells.
- **Recommended tier:** Revenue Diagnostic first. They don't need a from-scratch Build (booking exists); they need a paid-acquisition + speed-to-lead diagnostic to find and plug the traffic/leak gap. Easy upsell to a managed Google Ads + SMS build if the diagnostic shows headroom.
- **Diligence flags:** (1) No verifiable TDLR/TACLB license number found — confirm before any compliance claims (and do NOT confuse with unrelated "Modern Air Mechanical LLC" TACLB94688E). (2) Texas entity/formation unverified via primary source. (3) Two live domains (modernairofkaty.com + modernair-katy.com) — possible SEO authority split. (4) Already tooled-up (HouseCall Pro/Trustindex) — less greenfield, more "optimize," which may reduce deal size.
- **Fit score rationale:** Owner-operator (+), reachable (charles@, phone, social) (+), affluent target geo (Memorial/Energy Corridor) (+), high-ticket vertical (+) — but already has booking + reviews + tooling (−), and no confirmed acquisition gap they can't partly self-serve (−). Net: a solid Diagnostic fit, not a slam-dunk greenfield Build.

## 9. Sources

See the Sources list field for all numbered URLs.

dossier.name: Evergreen Outdoor Services, LLC (dba "Evergreen Lawn Care, LLC")
dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

**MAJOR SEED CORRECTION.** The seed describes a thin, Facebook-only, "~89 likes / minimal web presence / west-side" early-stage lead. That is **wrong**. The Facebook page in the seed (facebook.com/EGLCHTX, branded "Evergreen Lawn Care, LLC") is the social handle for an **established, fast-growing, well-reviewed company: Evergreen Outdoor Services, LLC**. (VERIFIED — Facebook page, BBB profile, company website all tie EGLCHTX / "Evergreen Lawn Care" branding to Evergreen Outdoor Services, LLC at the same phone (832) 506-8239 and website evergreenlawncaretx.com.)

- **Legal name:** Evergreen Outdoor Services, LLC. Markets/brands under "Evergreen Lawn Care" / "Evergreen Outdoor Services." (VERIFIED — BBB)
- **Vertical:** Residential + light-commercial lawn care, landscaping, sod, mulch, drainage, pressure washing, seasonal (incl. Christmas lights). (VERIFIED — website, Nextdoor)
- **Service area:** HQ is **NORTHEAST Houston, NOT west side.** Address 11922 Green River Dr, Houston, TX 77044-2512 (Summerwood); Google Maps coords ~29.838, -95.224 confirm NE. (VERIFIED — BBB, Google Maps). Core/origin market: Summerwood, Kingwood, Atascocita, Humble, Crosby, CE King. However, their marketing site actively targets **affluent west/central neighborhoods too** — Memorial Villages, River Oaks, Tanglewood, Galleria/Greater Uptown, West University, Bellaire, Heights, Montrose. (VERIFIED — website service-area copy). So the "west side" seed note is half-right only as an aspirational marketing target, not their base.
- **Texas entity status:** Operating as an LLC; **business start date October 1, 2020** per BBB. (VERIFIED — BBB lists "Date of New Ownership/Business Started: 10/1/2020", entity type LLC.) Franchise-tax "active/good standing" status on the Texas Comptroller could **not** be confirmed this session — the Comptroller search is JS-rendered and did not return results via fetch. (UNVERIFIED — Comptroller status; needs a live lookup.)
- **License:** Lawn care/landscaping/mowing generally requires **no state occupational license in Texas** (no TDLR/TDA license needed unless they apply restricted-use pesticides, which would require a TDA license — no evidence either way). So "n/a" is the expected answer. (UNVERIFIED that they hold/need any license — none found, none likely required.)
- **Active confirmation:** Website live and content-rich; phone (832) 506-8239 published across BBB, Yelp, Nextdoor, website; recent posts (Yelp "Updated May 2026"; FB posts current). Business is clearly **active and operating**. (VERIFIED)

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

This is the heart of the demotion. Reputation is **strong and growing fast** — the opposite of an unproven startup.

- **Google:** 4.9 stars, ~430–458 reviews (aggregators report 430; Birdeye mirror shows 458 Google reviews). GBP is **claimed/live** (own Google Maps place page with photos, hours, and a populated place ID g/11qlbz22fq). (VERIFIED rating + GBP existence; exact count needs live confirmation — figures range 430–458 across sources.)
- **Review velocity (key signal):** A Dec 4, 2024 PR cited "**over 160 five-star reviews**." Aggregators now show ~430–458. That implies roughly **+270 reviews in ~18 months (~15/mo)** — a business with real, scaling demand. (VERIFIED trend from two dated sources; exact velocity UNVERIFIED.)
- **BBB:** **Accredited, A+ rating**, accredited since 3/27/2024, file opened 3/26/2024. No complaint themes surfaced. (VERIFIED — BBB)
- **Yelp:** Live listing (22 photos, "Updated May 2026"); review count/rating not extracted (Yelp blocked direct fetch). (UNVERIFIED count.)
- **Facebook (EGLCHTX):** Active page with current posts. The seed's "~89 likes" is stale/incorrect for an entity at this scale; actual like/follower count not confirmed this session. (UNVERIFIED count.)
- **Nextdoor:** Active business page (under "Jorge Perez"), shows recommendations and active owner posting; ~3 recommendations surfaced (low — Nextdoor is an under-leveraged channel for them). (VERIFIED page exists; count UNVERIFIED.)
- **Instagram:** @evergreenlawncare.tx (Jorge Perez) active; metrics not extracted. TikTok, YouTube, LinkedIn also linked. (VERIFIED presence.)
- **Review THEMES — top praise (proof it's worth scaling):** professionalism, fast response, reliability ("show up when we say"), clear communication, "restored my yard to life," uniformed crews, quality materials, "no surprises." (VERIFIED — BBB excerpt, aggregator summaries.) **Complaints:** none material surfaced this session. (UNVERIFIED absence — needs a live read of 1–2★ Google/Yelp reviews.)

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

This is where a genuine Zapp opportunity still exists despite the demotion — the brand and demand are strong, but the conversion path is **manual/consultative, not frictionless**.

- **Website:** YES — evergreenlawncaretx.com. **WordPress** (confirmed via /wp-content/ artifacts). Not a thin UENI/templated one-pager — it's a **multi-page SEO build** with neighborhood landing pages (Greater Uptown, Humble, etc.), a blog, service pages, commercial page. (VERIFIED) Mobile experience not directly tested this session (UNVERIFIED, but WordPress + modern theme implies responsive).
- **Booking: NO online booking / no scheduling calendar / no instant-quote tool.** The entire conversion path is **quote-request only**: contact form (/contact-evergreen-outdoor/), phone, and "**text us for a quote**" SMS link. Heavy "Request Your Lawn Care Quote" / "Get My Fast Quote" CTAs everywhere — but every one funnels to a manual human quote, not a booked job. (VERIFIED)
- **Pricing:** Partial transparency (small lawns "from $50/visit"; one-time cuts "$125 min"; standard residential ~$50–85/visit) but **no online quote calculator or self-serve checkout**. (VERIFIED)
- **Payment:** Recurring clients are put on "**secure auto-pay / card on file**" — so they DO have card-on-file billing for retained clients, but there's **no online payment/booking for new customers** (no pay-to-book). (VERIFIED — website)
- **SMS/automation:** They invite inbound texts and run "referral + loyalty perks," but there's **no evidence of automated SMS lead-follow-up** (e.g., instant auto-reply, missed-lead nurture, quote-to-close drip). Follow-up appears manual. (UNVERIFIED automation — none detected.)
- **Lead-capture quality:** Decent top-of-funnel (strong SEO + many CTAs) but the **handoff is the leak**: every lead requires Jorge/Jeffrey or a team member to manually quote, then manually follow up, then manually book. No instant-quote, no online calendar, no automated speed-to-lead.

**SINGLE BIGGEST CONVERSION LEAK:** **No online booking + no automated speed-to-lead follow-up.** They drive strong demand (450+ reviews, top SEO) into a manual quote queue. Every minute of quote-response delay and every un-nurtured form/text is lost revenue — exactly the gap Zapp closes (frictionless booking + Twilio SMS auto-follow-up). The Royal Pawz analogy (7%→30.8% booking conversion) maps directly: convert their high-intent quote requests into booked, calendared jobs instead of a manual back-and-forth.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads now:** **None detected** in search results; no sponsored placement surfaced. (UNVERIFIED — needs live Google Ads Transparency Center check.) Their visibility appears **organic/SEO-driven**, not paid.
- **Meta Ad Library:** No active ads confirmed (Ad Library is JS-gated; direct fetch failed). (UNVERIFIED — needs live Ad Library check on Page EGLCHTX.)
- **Platform dependency:** Present on HomeAdvisor/Angi, Yelp, Thumbtack-style aggregators, Nextdoor — but does **not** appear dependent on paid platform leads; primary engine is owned SEO + word-of-mouth/referrals. (VERIFIED presence; paid-platform spend UNVERIFIED.)
- **SEO footprint: STRONG.** This is the standout. They rank/own content for "Houston lawn care," neighborhood-specific pages, a blog, and have placed **multiple PR newswires** (KingNewswire Dec 2024, OpenPR). They are **not invisible — they're an SEO leader** for an SMB this size. (VERIFIED)
- **Pixels:** Google Analytics/Maps integration visible; Meta pixel not confirmed. (UNVERIFIED Meta pixel.)

**Implication for Zapp:** They have already solved demand-gen (the hard part for most Zapp leads). The opportunity is **conversion + retention infrastructure**, not lead-gen — which reframes the pitch from "we'll get you leads" to "you're already getting leads; you're leaking them at the booking step."

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Owners:** **Jorge Perez & Jeffrey Perez** (brothers / family-run). Jorge is the public face (LinkedIn: "General Manager at Evergreen Outdoor Services"); BBB lists both as Partners. (VERIFIED — BBB, LinkedIn, PR.)
- **Origin story:** Jorge grew up in Houston, started mowing yards on weekends in 2020, built it via word-of-mouth into a family team and one-truck-to-multi-crew operation. (VERIFIED — multiple sources.) Strong **family-owned, founder-operator, likely bilingual (Hispanic-owned)** angles — good rapport fit for Zapp.
- **Direct contact:** (832) 506-8239 (call/text — the owner answers leads here); contact form at evergreenlawncaretx.com/contact-evergreen-outdoor/; Instagram @evergreenlawncare.tx; LinkedIn /in/jorge-perez-9433a9159. (VERIFIED)
- **Personality signals:** Marketing-savvy and growth-minded — actively posts on Nextdoor/IG, runs referral+loyalty programs, invests in PR and SEO, uses "increasing property values / sustainable practices" positioning. Responds to/encourages reviews (driving 4.9 across 450+). This is a **sophisticated, ambitious operator** — will respond well to ROI/systems framing, but is **not** a naive "doesn't have a website" lead; expect them to already know their numbers.

## 6. Competitive landscape

West/central Houston lawn-and-landscape is crowded with booking-equipped incumbents and tech platforms:

- **GreenBee Lawn Care** (greenbeelawn.com) — explicitly targets Memorial / Energy Corridor / West Houston; offers **same-day quotes and 48-hour service**, fast online flow. Has the speed-to-service Evergreen lacks. (VERIFIED)
- **LawnStarter / GreenPal** — app/online **instant booking in <2 min**, self-serve scheduling and payment. This is the exact frictionless-booking benchmark Evergreen is missing. (VERIFIED)
- **The Grounds Guys of Memorial Park** (national franchise) + **Hogue Landscape Services** (high-end River Oaks/Memorial/Bellaire design) + **SWP Landscaping** (30+ yrs) — strong brand/SEO and, for the franchises, online request systems. (VERIFIED)

**What Evergreen lacks vs. these:** instant online booking, self-serve scheduling, online payment-to-book, and automated speed-to-lead. **Where Evergreen can win:** it already beats most of them on **review volume + rating (4.9 / 450+) and owner-operator trust/communication**. If it bolts a frictionless booking + SMS-automation layer onto that reputation, it can out-convert the platforms (which lack the human trust) and the franchises (which lack the review density). That's a realistic, defensible wedge.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket sizes (VERIFIED from their own site / vertical norms):** recurring mow $50–85/visit; one-time cut $125 min; sod/mulch/drainage/landscape projects run **$1,500–$10,000+**. The real money is recurring maintenance contracts (annualized $1,200–$2,500+/client) and larger landscape/install/drainage jobs.
- **Paid-search economics (vertical benchmark, West Houston, UNVERIFIED estimate):** lawn/landscape CPCs ~$6–18; cost-per-lead ~$30–90; with a strong site + booking, blended CAC for a recurring client can land $80–200. On an annualized maintenance LTV of $1,200–2,500+, a tuned funnel clears **3–6x+ ROAS** — comparable to the Royal Pawz 5x proof point.
- **Seasonality:** Houston is near year-round mowing (mild winters), with spring/summer peak (Mar–Sep) and a Q4 leaf-cleanup + Christmas-lights bump. Less seasonal trough than northern markets — favorable for always-on acquisition.
- **Royal-Pawz-style conversion-lift math (illustrative, UNVERIFIED inputs):** Say they currently field ~150 quote requests/month (plausible given 450+ reviews + SEO + 15 reviews/mo). If manual quoting converts ~25% to booked jobs (~37 jobs) and a frictionless booking + Twilio speed-to-lead system lifts that to ~45% (~67 jobs) — that's **+30 booked jobs/month**. At a conservative blended first-job value of $300 (mix of recurring + project), that's **~+$9,000/month (~+$108k/yr)** in net-new booked revenue, before counting the recurring LTV tail of the new maintenance clients. Even a modest 25%→35% lift is **~+$4,500/mo**. The leverage is real because demand already exists — Zapp would be monetizing leads they're currently leaking, not buying new ones.

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why them / why now (one line):** A 4.9-star, 450-review, fast-scaling family lawn brand that already wins the hard battle (demand + reputation) but pours every high-intent lead into a **manual quote-and-text queue** — the exact conversion + speed-to-lead leak Zapp plugged for Royal Pawz.
- **The lead gap:** No online booking, no automated SMS follow-up, no self-serve scheduling/pay-to-book — high intent in, manual friction out.
- **Recommended Zapp tier: Revenue Diagnostic first** (then Build). They're sophisticated and already invest in marketing, so lead with a diagnostic that quantifies their actual quote→booked conversion and speed-to-lead, then propose the booking + Twilio build. A cold "we'll build you a funnel" pitch underrates them; a "here's exactly where you're leaking booked revenue" pitch lands.
- **Fit score rationale (58):** DEMOTED from a typical early-stage Zapp target. Positives: reachable owner-operator, real recurring/high-ticket economics, genuine booking/automation gap, affluent west-side aspirations, family-owned rapport. Negatives that cap the score: this is an **established, SEO-strong, 450+ review, PR-active, BBB-A+ brand** — more mature and marketing-savvy than Zapp's sweet spot; they may already have an agency or in-house capability, and won't be impressed by basic lead-gen claims. They also already do card-on-file auto-pay (some infra maturity).
- **Diligence flags:** (1) Confirm Comptroller franchise-tax active status. (2) Confirm they aren't already running Google/Meta ads or working with an agency. (3) Confirm exact current Google review count + scan 1–2★ reviews for any service/billing complaints. (4) Verify whether the "west-side" service is real fulfillment or just SEO targeting (affects ROI geo framing — anchor on Memorial/Briar Forest/Energy Corridor only if they truly serve there).

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

- **Legal/brand name:** Katy Brothers Pressure Washing. Operating brand consistent across all platforms. The exact registered legal entity (LLC vs sole prop) could NOT be confirmed -- the Texas Comptroller Franchise Tax search is form-only (no constructable URL), and Bizapedia/OpenCorporates were CAPTCHA/gated this session. (UNVERIFIED legal entity)
- **Vertical:** Pressure washing / exterior cleaning. Service menu (VERIFIED from both sites): house soft washing, roof cleaning, driveway & sidewalk washing, pool deck, porch/patio, fence & deck, parking-lot power washing, and HOA/commercial power washing.
- **Service area (VERIFIED from sites):** Katy, Fulshear, Brookshire, Richmond, Cypress, Memorial, Spring, Sugar Land, Houston. This explicitly includes the affluent **Memorial** corridor -- the right ROI-framing geography. No specific ZIP list published.
- **Established:** Company self-reports "established 2019" (UNVERIFIED -- single-source, the company's own Hibu site). That ~6-7 year tenure is consistent with the review volume below.
- **License + status:** Texas does NOT license residential pressure washing as an occupation, so there is no TDLR/TCEQ/TDA license to verify. The site's claim "Licensed by the State of Texas and backed by a $2 million insurance policy" most likely refers to general business/sales-tax registration plus private liability insurance, not an occupational license. (UNVERIFIED; treat the "$2M policy" as a marketing claim until proven.) Note: the about page renders the figure as "$2,00,000" -- a typo for $2,000,000 -- a small credibility/site-quality tell.
- **Active confirmation:** Both websites are LIVE (VERIFIED -- fetched content successfully). Phone 281-736-5800 is the published contact/text line (a second number, 281-533-3142, also appears on the Hibu site). Email russell@katybrothers.com is published. Facebook, Nextdoor, Instagram, and a YouTube channel all exist. Business is clearly active and operating.
- **Notable: there are TWO live websites** -- `katybrotherspressurewashingtx.com` (built on **Hibu**, payment-methods + service-area heavy) and `katybrothers.com` (**WordPress**). The WordPress site shows a leftover **restaurant template artifact** ("Discover Katy Brothers - Exceptional Cuisine" header) -- a clear sign of a low-effort/templated build that was never fully cleaned up. This split/duplicate web presence is itself a problem (SEO cannibalization, brand confusion, NAP inconsistency).

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

- **Google:** A third-party Katy pressure-washing directory (pressurewasherstexas.com) lists Katy Brothers at **5.0/5 with 123 reviews** (UNVERIFIED -- single-source aggregator; not yet confirmed directly on Google Maps, and GBP claim status unknown). If accurate, this is a substantial, well-established review base -- NOT an invisible early-stage operator.
- **Facebook:** "100% recommend / 18 reviews," ~368-424 followers reported across snippets (UNVERIFIED). A "Coming Soon" reel exists; cadence appears light.
- **Nextdoor:** Two page variants exist; one reported ~**13 faves**, 5.0, with consistent neighbor praise (UNVERIFIED). NOTE: the prospecting seed claimed "5 Faves" -- that appears **stale/understated**; real footprint is larger.
- **Yelp:** Listing exists but returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch -- count/rating not retrieved (must be checked live).
- **Instagram (@katy_brothers_):** ~26 followers, 7 posts -- effectively **dormant** (UNVERIFIED). YouTube channel exists but appears minimal.
- **Review THEMES (proof it's worth scaling):** Recurring praise = Russell is personally communicative and pleasant; punctual/professional; thorough; results "look brand new"; and repeatedly "**very reasonable / unbeatable price**." Customers also note it was "**easy to make appointments over text**." No complaints surfaced.
- **Read-through:** The reputation is genuine and the service quality is high (great raw material for scaling). The "very reasonable price" theme is a double edge -- strong word-of-mouth driver but suggests under-pricing / margin left on the table.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

- **Website(s):** Two live sites. `katybrothers.com` = WordPress (with an un-removed restaurant template header). `katybrotherspressurewashingtx.com` = Hibu. Both are templated, brochure-style, with minimal conversion engineering.
- **Mobile experience:** Not load-tested live this session (flag for live check), but Hibu/WordPress templated builds are typically passable-but-generic; nothing custom for conversion.
- **Booking:** **Form-only + phone + manual text quote.** Primary CTA on every page is "Request Quote." There is **NO online booking, NO calendar, NO instant scheduling** (VERIFIED on both sites). Quoting is manual and (per reviews) text/photo-based.
- **Payment:** Broad list on the Hibu site -- Amex, Cash, Check, Discover, Invoice, MasterCard, PayPal, Visa, Zelle. No evidence of integrated online/up-front checkout or deposits; payment appears to happen after the manual quote.
- **SMS/email automation:** None detected. Follow-up is Russell doing it by hand. No auto-reply/confirmation evident on the quote form (verify live).
- **Lead-capture quality:** Weak. A static quote form with no instant scheduling, no auto-response, and no nurture means speed-to-lead depends entirely on the owner's availability -- exactly where the established incumbents win (ReadyKleen reviewers report a callback "within 2 minutes").
- **Tracking:** No Google Analytics or Meta Pixel detected in fetched HTML (UNVERIFIED -- confirm live). Without pixels, they cannot retarget or measure.
- **SINGLE BIGGEST CONVERSION LEAK:** **No instant online booking + no automated speed-to-lead.** A reputation that generates ~120 five-star reviews is funneling every prospect into "text Russell and wait." Visitors who won't text, or who text after-hours, are lost to competitors who book or call back instantly. The fix is squarely Zapp's core offer.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads:** None detected. Katy Brothers does not appear in paid or top-organic positions for core terms ("pressure washing Katy TX," "soft wash Katy") -- those SERPs are dominated by incumbents and other operators. (UNVERIFIED-negative -- confirm with a live branded/non-branded SERP and the Google Ads Transparency Center.)
- **Meta ads:** No confirmed active ads. The Meta Ad Library is JS-gated and could not be fetched; one search snippet loosely claimed "advertisements found on Facebook" but that is unreliable inference, not Ad-Library confirmation. Treat as **none detected** pending live check.
- **Platform dependency:** Primary demand engine is **organic word-of-mouth / Nextdoor / Google reviews** -- not paid. No evidence of Yelp Ads, Angi, Thumbtack, or HomeAdvisor spend.
- **Pixels:** None detected on either site -- so no retargeting infrastructure exists.
- **SEO footprint:** Weak relative to incumbents, and likely self-cannibalized by running two domains for one brand. They rank on their brand name but appear **invisible for high-intent non-branded terms** -- the most valuable searches go to competitors.

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Owner:** **Russell Harms** (VERIFIED -- email russell@katybrothers.com + LinkedIn "Russell Harms - Katy Brothers Pressure Washing"). A second LinkedIn ("Russell Test Account - Owner - Katy Brothers Pressure Washing - Strategic Power Washing Solutions For Commercial Owners & Managers") suggests he's actively positioning toward **commercial/HOA B2B accounts** -- a growth intent signal worth leaning into.
- **Role:** Owner-operator. He is named personally throughout reviews ("Russell went above and beyond," "Russell just cleaned our driveway") -- this is a true one-operator, relationship-driven business.
- **Direct contact:** 281-736-5800 (text-friendly), russell@katybrothers.com.
- **Personality signals:** Highly responsive, personable, community-minded ("We live here... we love our community," sponsors local businesses incl. a bakery and a Greek restaurant). Family-owned positioning is consistent across platforms. This personal, hands-on style is the asset -- and the bottleneck (everything routes through him).
- **Angles:** Family-/owner-operated; community-rooted; explicit interest in commercial/HOA expansion -- a natural fit for a "let the system handle intake so you can sell the big accounts" pitch.

## 6. Competitive landscape

West Houston / Katy pressure washing is competitive and review-heavy. Dominant, SEO-forward incumbents:

- **Cypress Pro Wash** (~437 reviews, 5.0) -- multi-location SEO site with dedicated Katy landing page, fast online estimate requests, broad service-area pages (Cypress, Katy, Spring, The Woodlands, Tomball). Reviewers cite quick online-inquiry responses and estimates within hours.
- **ReadyKleen Power Washing** (~362-385 reviews, 5.0; Katy/77494) -- veteran-owned, easy web request form, and reviewers report **callbacks "within 2 minutes."** Strong speed-to-lead.
- **PureView / NOVA / Katy Memorial Services** and several "Brothers"-named competitors (Brothers Pressure Washing, Arms Brothers) cluster in the 60-230 review range, some clearly running social ads/reels.

**What they have that Katy Brothers lacks:** dedicated SEO landing pages per city, instant estimate/booking funnels, faster speed-to-lead, retargeting pixels, and (for some) active paid social. **Where Katy Brothers can realistically win:** it already has a top-tier 5.0 reputation and ~120 reviews -- on par or close to the leaders on trust. The gap is purely **conversion infrastructure and top-of-funnel paid traffic**, not reputation. Adding instant booking + speed-to-lead + a single clean SEO site + a modest Google Ads engine would let it convert its existing brand equity instead of losing leads to faster-responding rivals. That is the most "winnable" gap of any prospect type.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket size (VERIFIED-ish from sites/market):** Driveways advertised "starting at $89"; Katy market driveway jobs run ~$80-$200, full house soft-wash + roof + flatwork bundles commonly $300-$700+. Blended residential ticket ~**$250-$400**; HOA/commercial jobs much larger.
- **Paid-search CAC / ROAS (vertical benchmark, UNVERIFIED for this operator):** Pressure-washing Google Ads in suburban Houston typically run roughly $8-$20/click with ~5-12% landing-to-lead and decent close rates on warm exterior-cleaning intent; well-run campaigns target ~3-5x ROAS. Speed-to-lead and a tight landing page are the dominant levers -- exactly what's missing here.
- **Seasonality:** Strong spring-summer demand (pollen, mildew, pre-listing/curb-appeal); softer in winter. Texas heat extends the season vs. northern markets.
- **Royal-Pawz-style lift math (illustrative, UNVERIFIED inputs):** Say the two sites pull ~250 monthly visitors and currently convert ~8% to a quote request via the text/form path (~20 leads). Add instant online booking + auto speed-to-lead SMS and lift effective booking conversion toward ~20-25% (the Royal Pawz pattern of roughly doubling-plus). That's ~30-40 more booked jobs' worth of pipeline; at a ~$300 blended ticket, even **+15 incremental booked jobs/month = ~$4,500/mo (~$54K/yr)** of recovered revenue from traffic they already have -- before adding any paid traffic on top. Layering a $1-2K/mo Google Ads engine at 4x ROAS adds another ~$4-8K/mo. The conversion fix alone likely pays for the engagement.

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why them / why now:** A genuinely well-reviewed (~120 x 5.0), community-trusted operator whose growth is capped by manual, owner-dependent intake -- no online booking, no follow-up automation, no paid traffic, and a messy two-site/leftover-template web presence. The reputation proves the service scales; only the system is missing. Owner is also signaling commercial/HOA ambition (needs the back-office to scale).
- **The lead gap:** Demand exists and is being *generated* (reviews, word-of-mouth) but not *captured* -- every lead bottlenecks through "text Russell." Speed-to-lead and instant booking are the leak.
- **Recommended tier: Revenue Diagnostic first.** Because this is an **established brand (demotion applies)**, lead with a paid diagnostic to (a) confirm the real Google review count/GBP status live, (b) quantify current site traffic/conversion, and (c) consolidate the two-domain mess -- then upsell into a System Build (booking + speed-to-lead SMS + single SEO site + Google Ads). It's clearly *Build-eligible*; Diagnostic just de-risks the established-operator profile and sizes the prize before committing.
- **Fit score 52 / MODERATE rationale:** Pros -- owner-operator, perfect affluent service area (Memorial), high-ticket-capable (HOA/commercial), reachable owner (Russell), thin conversion infra, no paid acquisition (greenfield upside). Cons / **demotion** -- this is NOT an early-stage invisible lead; ~120 reviews + 6-7 years in business means he already has a working word-of-mouth machine and may be price-sensitive / "if it ain't broke" minded; budget/sophistication unknown; entity unverified. The established footprint lowers urgency (hence ~52, not 70+), but the textbook conversion-infra gap keeps it a real, workable fit.
- **Diligence flags:** (1) ~123-review count is single-source -- verify on Google Maps live. (2) Two live domains -- confirm which is canonical and whether NAP is consistent. (3) "Established 2019" and "$2M insurance / licensed by Texas" are self-reported -- verify entity via Comptroller/SOS. (4) Confirm zero paid ads via Meta Ad Library + Google Ads Transparency live. (5) Possible price-sensitivity given the heavy "very reasonable price" review theme.

Instant price -> select service -> schedule -> book is all online and self-service, 24/7.
- **Lead capture:** Multiple CTAs — "Get Your Instant Quote Now / INSTANT ONLINE QUOTE," "BOOK ONLINE," click-to-call phone, plus a WPForms contact form. Customer login portal exists (recurring-client management). High-quality capture.
- **Payment:** Could not positively confirm the on-page payment processor in the embedded short-form (no Stripe/Square string detected in the snippet captured) — BookingKoala natively supports card capture/Stripe, so online card payment is **probable but UNVERIFIED**.

- **SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** Not the on-site funnel (it's already strong). The real leak is **demand volume into a well-built funnel that is starved of paid traffic** — no detectable paid acquisition feeding the BookingKoala flow, plus **no Meta pixel** so they can't retarget the quote-abandoners they already attract. Secondary leak: no evident owned speed-to-lead SMS on form/quote abandons.

## 6. Competitive landscape

West Houston / Energy Corridor maid-service is crowded and SEO-heavy:

- **Superb Maids Houston** — dedicated Energy Corridor page, online scheduling, strong SEO.
- **Naturalcare Cleaning Service** — established, eco-positioned, easy online booking, deep Houston footprint.
- **MoreHands Maid Service** — long-standing Houston brand with broad city coverage.
- **National aggregators** — Cleanzen ("$89"), Handy, Homeaglow, Hellamaid — price-led, heavy paid-search spenders that dominate top-of-funnel ad inventory.

What competitors have that Sunrise lacks: **paid-search/paid-social muscle and retargeting infrastructure** (the aggregators outspend everyone on Google; Sunrise has no detected paid layer or Meta pixel). What **Sunrise already matches or beats them on:** a genuine instant-quote + online-booking funnel (BookingKoala), a flawless 5.0 review wall, and a credible local/community brand. **Where Sunrise can realistically win:** convert its premium, trusted, Katy-anchored reputation into the affluent Energy Corridor/Memorial/Briar Forest core via paid acquisition + retargeting — markets where its quality story beats the $89 discounters and where it currently has only a thin landing-page presence.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket size (their own pricing):** maid service from **$150**, deep clean from **$200**; recurring clients are the profit core (VERIFIED — site pricing). A West-Houston affluent home (Memorial/Briar Forest/Energy Corridor, larger sq ft) realistically runs **~$180–$300 per recurring visit**, and a biweekly recurring client is worth **~$4,000–$7,000+ in annual revenue** and multi-year LTV.
- **Paid-search economics (vertical benchmark, UNVERIFIED to this firm):** Houston house-cleaning Google Ads CPCs commonly run ~$6–$15; with a tuned instant-quote funnel, booked-customer CAC typically lands in the **~$80–$200** range. Against a multi-thousand-dollar recurring LTV, even conservative paid acquisition clears 3–5x+ ROAS — the same shape as the Royal Pawz outcome.
- **Seasonality:** Spring (deep-clean season), pre-holiday (Oct–Dec), and move season (summer) spike demand; Jan/late-summer soften. A paid layer lets them buy demand in the troughs.
- **Royal-Pawz-style math (illustrative, their dollars):** Their funnel is already built, so the lever is **volume**, not conversion rate. If Zapp drives an incremental **20 booked recurring clients/month** via paid + retargeting at ~$120 CAC, and each recurring client is worth ~$5,000/yr, that's **~$100,000 in new annual recurring revenue for ~$2,400/mo media** — a ~40x revenue-to-spend ratio before churn. Even discounting heavily, the ROI case is strong because the conversion infrastructure they'd normally pay Zapp to build **already exists** — Zapp's value here is the **traffic + retargeting + speed-to-lead layer on top**, not a ground-up build.

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why them / why now:** A 6-year, 5.0-rated, professionally-systematized Katy cleaner that already runs the full instant-quote + online-booking funnel — but is **starved of paid demand and can't even retarget** (no Meta pixel, no detected paid spend). Their proven service quality + premium brand is the perfect engine to point at the affluent Energy Corridor/Memorial core they only lightly touch today.
- **The lead gap:** Great funnel, thin top-of-funnel. They convert organic/referral traffic beautifully but aren't buying demand, and they're leaving retargetable visitors on the table.
- **Recommended Zapp tier:** **Revenue Diagnostic first.** Because the build is already done, a full Revenue System Build is partly redundant — lead with a diagnostic that quantifies their current paid gap and models the Energy-Corridor ROI, then upsell a **paid-acquisition + retargeting + speed-to-lead SMS** engagement (a "growth/run" scope rather than a "build" scope).
- **Diligence flags:** (1) Established, not early-stage — demotion applied. (2) Owner-name reconciliation: Adam Dolch (public co-owner) vs. James Petty (BBB president) vs. "Sunrise Venture Group" parent — clarify the real decision-maker/structure before pitching.
